title: China launches new highly retrograde Yaogan satellite, KZ-11 rideshare deploys 8 satellites
summary:
Here is a concise summary of the space industry news:

China conducted two successful launches on Sunday, including the deployment of a second Yaogan-50 satellite into a highly retrograde orbit. This matters because the Yaogan-50 satellite's unique orbit and mission requirements demonstrate China's growing capabilities in developing complex spacecraft and expanding its presence in Earth observation and surveillance missions.

title: Starcloud files plans for 88,000-satellite constellation
summary:
Here is a summary of the space industry news in 2 concise sentences:

Starcloud, an orbital data center startup, has filed with the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to deploy a massive constellation of up to 88,000 satellites. This enormous project would provide unprecedented global connectivity and access to satellite-based services, potentially revolutionizing the way we communicate and use data in space.

title: OSTP taking on space policy coordination work in place of National Space Council
summary:
Here is a 2-sentence summary:

The White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) has taken the lead role in coordinating national space policy, filling the gap left by the absence of the National Space Council. This shift matters because OSTP's involvement ensures continued coordination and guidance on national space efforts, despite the hiatus in the National Space Council.

title: Volunteers Find Oddly High Solar Flare Rates
summary:
Here is a summary of the article in 2 concise sentences:

A new study has shed light on "long-lived active regions" on the Sun's surface, which are areas with strong magnetic fields that can emerge and decay over days, weeks, or even months. This research matters because it helps scientists better understand these patches, which can have a significant impact on space weather and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Extra Extra! Extra Data Stream Added to the Daily Minor Planet!
summary:
Here is a summary of the space industry news:

The Daily Minor Planet citizen science project is expanding its scope, incorporating data from the Bok 2.3-meter telescope at Kitt Peak National Observatory in addition to its existing nightly feed from the Catalina Sky Survey's Mt. Lemmon telescope in Arizona. This expansion matters because it will enable scientists and citizens alike to contribute to a broader understanding of minor planets and potentially discover new ones, advancing our knowledge of the solar system.

title: NASA Selects Finalists in Student Aircraft Maintenance Competition
summary:
Here is a summary of the space industry news in 2 concise sentences:

NASA has selected eight student teams as finalists in the 2026 Gateways to Blue Skies Competition, aiming to address the critical challenge of maintenance in the commercial aviation industry. This initiative matters because it taps into student innovation to solve pressing issues like the shortage of qualified maintenance workers and increasing demands to keep complex aircraft running for longer.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary:
Here is a summary of the ESA's "Delivers" booklet in 2 concise sentences:

The European Space Agency (ESA) has released a commemorative booklet celebrating its 50-year history, highlighting 50 hallmark achievements that showcase its notable contributions to space exploration. This milestone publication matters because it provides a comprehensive look at the agency's significant accomplishments and highlights its enduring impact on advancing our understanding of space and the universe.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary:
Here is a summary of the article in 2 concise sentences:

The European Space Agency's (ESA) mission to discover Earth-like exoplanets, Plato, has been placed in ESA's Test Centre's Large Space Simulator (LSS) chamber for vital tests under space-like conditions. This simulated environment allows the spacecraft to be tested for functionality and performance before its planned launch by the end of 2027, ensuring that it is ready to successfully detect terrestrial planets orbiting Sun-like stars.
